title: Trump caught unprepared by escalation of war with Iran
subhed: The American president has made a series of contradictory statements and is struggling to justify the joint US-Israeli intervention to the public, especially as the closure of the Strait of Hormuz and strikes on energy sites in the Gulf have fueled inflation.

title: France's aircraft carrier located in real time by Le Monde through Strava data

Yazidi genocide trial in Paris: Prosecutors seek life sentence for French jihadist
subhed: The prosecutor at the trial over IS group crimes against the Middle Eastern Yazidi community, the first of its kind in France, requested life imprisonment for French jihadist Sabri Essid, who stands accused of buying and raping Yazidi sex slaves.

title: Bertrand Badie, political scientist: 'Persian culture is an impetuous alliance of dignity and fury'
subhed: The international relations specialist, who is of Iranian descent on his father's side, speaks with Le Monde about the West's lack of understanding of Iran and its culture.

title: Anne-Claire Legendre, new president of France's Arab World Institute: 'Our role is to provide tools for understanding'
subhed: Legendre, who replaced former culture minister Jack Lang in February, speaks to Le Monde about her priorities for revitalizing the institution.

title: Richard Ford: 'The regime that most urgently needs changing is the one that sits in Washington'
subhed: 'Writers on the war in the Middle East' 1/4. Le Monde asked writers Richard Ford, Zeruya Shalev and Navid Kermani for their perspectives on the war.

How Budapest became a hub for Western nationalists under Viktor Orban
subhed: The Hungarian capital, popular among hard-right circles in the West, is home to a number of think tanks and conferences promoting the image of Prime Minister Viktor Orban with the approach of the legislative elections on April 12.

title: In Paris, Reza Pahlavi, son of Iran's last shah, builds his network
subhed: Reza Pahlavi has been based in the French capital since the US-Israeli strikes on Iran started, making moves in anticipation of the Islamic Republic's fall.

title: After Zohran Mamdani's election, New York's Muslim spirit shines bright
subhed: The election of the young Democrat, a Shia of Indian descent, has highlighted the vitality of the Muslim community in the nation's largest city. This new generation of believers, shaped by 9/11 and champions of a progressive Islam, is part of a cultural and intellectual ferment reminiscent of the emergence of New York's Jewish identity more than half a century ago.
